'''David Boyle''' (born 31 August 1959<ref>{{cite book| editor=Gary Lester |title=The Sun Book of Rugby League – 1983|year=1983|publisher=John Fairfax Marketing|location=[[Sydney]], New South Wales|isbn=0-909558-83-3|pages=78}}</ref>) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[New South Wales Rugby League]] (NSWRL) competition and for [[Hull FC]] in England.

==Playing career==
Boyle played in the [[Rugby league positions#Forwards|forwards]], starting in the second row and moving to the front row midway through his career, though he was lightweight even by the standards of the 1980s. His high workrate, however, made him part of one of the strongest defences in the NSWRL competition of the late 1980s under [[George Piggins]].

Boyle was selected to represent [[New South Wales state rugby league team|New South Wales]] as a forward for the three games of the [[1987 Rugby League State of Origin series|1987 State of Origin]] series as well as the fourth or 'exhibition' game of the year which was played at [[Veterans Memorial Stadium (Long Beach)|Veteran's Memorial Stadium]] in [[Long Beach, California]]. Boyle scored one State of Origin try in game III. A one-club player, Boyle spent ten seasons with South Sydney. He was part of the 1989 [[Minor Premiership]] winning team.

==Post playing==
Boyle later moved into sports fitness and worked with [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and then the Rabbitohs as a strength and conditioning coach.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Sports round-up|url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2004/07/13/1089694361749.html?from=moreStories|access-date=2007-11-17|date=2004-07-14}}</ref> David Boyle is a current board member of the Australian Strength & Conditioning Association.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Australian Strength & Conditioning Association|title=About-Us|url=http://strengthandconditioning.org/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=71&Itemid=469|access-date=2012-08-09|date=2012-08-09}}</ref>

In 2010, Boyle was the endorsed [[Australian Labor Party]] candidate for the NSW federal [[Division of Gilmore]]. On 18 June 2010, Boyle announced that he would withdraw as the endorsed candidate for Gilmore.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Illawarra Mercury|title=David Boyle to quit race for Gilmore|url=http://www.illawarramercury.com.au/news/local/news/general/david-boyle-to-quit-race-for-gilmore/1862264.aspx|access-date=2010-06-18|date=2010-06-18}}</ref> Later that year he was elected as a councillor for Shellharbour Council.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=South Coast Register|title=David Boyle gets start in politics|url=http://www.southcoastregister.com.au/news/local/news/general/david-boyle-gets-start-in-politics/2291135.aspx|access-date=18 March 2012|date=14 September 2011}}</ref>

In 2011, Boyle partnered with [http://www.protrainingprograms.com/ Pro Training Programs] to offer his rugby league training programs for non professional athletes.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Pro Training Programs|title=David Boyle Rugby Union Training Programs|url=http://www.protrainingprograms.com/trainers/david-boyle/|access-date=2012-08-09|date=2012-08-09}}</ref>
